task_completion.description: The Floor material is modified to a warm brown color with moderate roughness.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender Material Properties tab or Shader Editor, the material for the floor (e.g., 'woodFloor') has its Base Color set to a brown shade and Roughness adjusted (around 0.3 to 0.7). Alternatively, the 3D viewport shows the floor as brown instead of flat grey.

task_completion.description: The Wall material is modified to clean white paint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender Material Properties tab or Shader Editor, the material for the walls (e.g., 'beige_paintedPipe') has its Base Color set to white (high RGB values). Alternatively, the 3D viewport shows the walls as white instead of flat grey.

task_completion.description: The Desk material is modified to a natural brown wood tone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender Material Properties tab or Shader Editor, the material for the desks/tables (e.g., 'schoolDesk_blackPlastic') has its Base Color set to a brown shade. Alternatively, the 3D viewport shows the desks as brown instead of flat grey.

task_completion.description: The Glass material is modified to be transparent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender Material Properties tab or Shader Editor, the material for the windows/glass (e.g., 'frostedGlass') has its Transmission value increased (e.g., > 0.5) or Alpha decreased. Alternatively, the 3D viewport shows the windows as transparent instead of flat grey.

task_completion.description: The corrected scene is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender window title bar displays 'classroom_fixed.blend' or a 'Save As' dialog is visible showing the path '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/classroom_fixed.blend'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Blender GUI to modify materials.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Blender interface being actively used (e.g., clicking on materials, adjusting sliders in the Properties panel or Shader Editor), rather than just running a python script in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the material properties rather than just renaming objects or faking the visual output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Material Properties panel or Shader Editor nodes are visible with adjusted values for Base Color, Roughness, or Transmission.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file from within Blender.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' dialog or Blender's top menu (File -> Save As) is used, or the Blender window title updates to the new filename.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must fix four specific materials in the Blender scene. The actual material names in the file map to 'woodFloor' (Floor), 'beige_paintedPipe' (Wall), 'schoolDesk_blackPlastic' (Desk), and 'frostedGlass' (Glass). The Floor should have a brown Base Color (R:0.15-0.6, G:0.08-0.35, B:0.02-0.2) and Roughness between 0.3-0.7. The Wall should have a white Base Color (RGB all 0.7-1.0). The Desk should have a brown Base Color (R:0.2-0.6, G:0.1-0.4, B:0.02-0.2). The Glass must have a Transmission value of at least 0.5. The final file must be saved as '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/classroom_fixed.blend'.
